Tests should follow the AAA pattern ( http://jamescooke.info/arrange-act-assert-pattern-for-python-developers.html ). > Marking a method as private, conveys a strong enough signal to the consumer of the API, but we should not limit in addition her capability to understand the library.

This is not my experience.

> For instance, I had to read Configurable._load_config_files(filename, path) to see that the path arg in Configurable.load_config_file(filename.path) can be a list.

That is a failure of the documentation of the public API, not an indicator that the private implementation detail should be covered in the public documentation.

There are two *very* different goals of generated API docs:

1. how to use the library
2. how does the library work

Having a single document covering both causes confusion, and inevitably results in people confusing private APIs with public ones.

It is okay (debateable) to have generated documentation of the "internal implementation details" in the docs, but they should not be in the same place (especially not the same page) as the public, "how should I use this library" documentation. Given the general readability of Python, I find that the best balance is to only produce docs of the actual public APIs, and rely on reading source code for the internal implementation details.

Yes, I removed it completely. I will check if your example still works correctly. In the meantime my reasoning to remove this condition:

The problem with the incorrectly prepended YAML alias names only occurred if `compact` was true. `writeNode` is called with `compact = true` from lines [536](https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/blob/0215138ec880c1e0ebc93f5a8c94bb753f5a5456/lib/js-yaml/dumper.js#L536) and from line [625](https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/blob/0215138ec880c1e0ebc93f5a8c94bb753f5a5456/lib/js-yaml/dumper.js#L625) and line [830](https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/blob/0215138ec880c1e0ebc93f5a8c94bb753f5a5456/lib/js-yaml/dumper.js#L830).

`(0 === level ? '\n' : '')` fixes prepending the alias for top level objects, i.e. for calls from line 830. For example, this snippet ensures that we get

```yaml
&ref_0
- *ref_0
```

instead of

```yaml
&ref_0- *ref_0
```

The two other positions which called `writeNode` with `compact = true` were not handled correctly by this condition.

Since I set `compact` to `false` when an alias is prepended, I think that this condition is not necessary anymore. Without the removal of `(0 === level ? '\n' : '')` we would now actually get

```yaml
&ref_0

- *ref_0
```

(note the extra empty line)

Yeah, I know that the API expects the parameter to be named `ssh_public_key` now. But I think the command line flag should still be called `--customer-ssh-key`.

The reason for this is backwards compatibility. At some point in the future, we would change the default version from `v1.2` to `v1.3`.

If we change this command-line flag, all the command lines of the following form would need to be updated:
```
qds.py cluster create --label test_label
                      --access-key-id aki
                      --secret-access-key sak
                      --customer-ssh-key file
```
However, if we keep calling the command-line flag `--customer-ssh-key`, we would maintain command-line backwards compatibility.

I would also suggest that you go through this PR and look for other cases which may have introduced incompatibilities like this and fix them.
